description_long: This is a realistic crab with a robust, oval-shaped carapace featuring mottled brown and tan coloration, accented by darker and lighter patches. Its two prominent claws are asymmetrical, with one noticeably larger and more reddish than the other, and both are tipped with darker hues. The crab's legs are slender, jointed, and covered in a mix of tan and brown, with pointed tips for gripping surfaces. The eyes are set on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace, providing a wide field of vision. The underside and inner surfaces of the limbs are lighter, almost whitish, contrasting with the darker, patterned dorsal shell. The overall appearance suggests a hardy crustacean adapted for both aquatic and terrestrial environments, commonly found in coastal or urban waterfront areas.
description: A mottled brown crab with a robust oval carapace, two prominent claws (one larger and reddish), and jointed tan legs. Its eyes are set on short stalks, and the shell features a mix of dark and light patches.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crab's face, both claws (one larger and reddish), and the full spread of its legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the crab's profile, the curvature of the carapace, and the length of the legs and claws.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace, the symmetrical arrangement of the legs, and the absence of visible facial features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other profile, with the large reddish claw prominent and the segmentation of the legs visible.
